items.headline: Japan Lifts Postwar Ban on Exporting Lethal Weapons Including Fighter Jets
items.body: Japan's cabinet formally ended a decades-old prohibition on exporting lethal weapons, allowing sales of fighter jets, missiles, and destroyers to 17 partner countries. The shift marks the most significant rewrite of Japan's pacifist defence posture since 1945 and opens a new front in regional arms competition as China condemned the move publicly.

items.headline: UAE Dismantles Second Iran-Linked Terror Cell in Two Months
items.body: UAE security services publicly identified 27 individuals accused of planning coordinated terrorist and sabotage operations inside the country with alleged links to Iran's governing ideology. A separate Iran-linked network was broken up just weeks earlier, suggesting an accelerating covert campaign against Gulf state targets.

items.headline: Gunman Kills Canadian Tourist at Teotihuacan Pyramids in Mexico
items.body: A gunman opened fire from the Pyramid of the Moon at Mexico's Teotihuacan site, killing one Canadian woman and wounding 13 tourists before dying by suicide. The attack struck one of Latin America's most visited archaeological sites fewer than two months before Mexico co-hosts the FIFA World Cup.

items.headline: Virginia Voters Approve Map That Could Give Democrats 10 of 11 Congressional Seats
items.body: Virginia voters approved a ballot measure allowing state lawmakers to bypass the bipartisan redistricting commission and draw congressional districts more favourable to Democrats. The result escalates a nationwide redistricting contest that has already seen Texas, California, Missouri, and North Carolina redraw lines in rapid sequence.

meanwhile.text: Researchers reported that a new AI model predicted novel antibiotic structures that proved effective against drug-resistant bacteria in animal trials, published April 2026.

meanwhile.text: A large clinical study published April 2026 found continuous glucose monitoring in non-diabetic adults reduced cardiovascular risk markers significantly over 12 months.

meanwhile.text: A new cross-country survey published April 2026 found majority support for authoritarian governance among adults under 35 in four Southeast Asian nations.
